heading: State administrative expenses

(a). generally revised and restructured subsection and, among other changes, inserted formula for determining State allocations for administrative costs incurred under the program authorized by section 17 of the National School Lunch Act, authorized the State to transfer up to ten percent of any amounts allocated for administrative costs of the programs for which such funds were allocated, and authorized retention by the Secretary for the Secretary’s use in administering certain programs, allocations for such programs, under this section and section 13(k)(1) of the National School Lunch Act.Pub. L. 95–6271977—Subsecs. (a) to (i). added subsecs. (i) of this section.Pub. L. 95–1661970— inserted provisions authorizing Secretary to utilize funds appropriated under this section for advances for administrative expenses of any other designated State agency as well as for those of the State educational agency and in the case of either State agency, for its administrative expenses in supervising and giving technical assistance to service institutions as well as to local school districts.Pub. L. 91–2481968— inserted the programs under sections 1759a and 1761 of this title to the enumeration of programs in which appropriated funds could be used for administrative expenses of local school districts in supervising and giving technical assistance and added section 1761 to the enumeration of sections covering programs of additional activities under which funds could be advanced only in amounts and to the extent determined necessary by the Secretary.Pub. children.heading: Amount and allocation of funds
children.label: a Amount and allocation of funds

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Each fiscal year, the Secretary shall make available to the States for their administrative costs an amount equal to not less than 1½ percent of the Federal funds expended under sections 4, 11, and 17 of the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act [, 1759a, 1766] and 1772 and 1773 of this title during the second preceding fiscal year.42 U.S.C. 1753

children.heading: Allocation
children.label: B Allocation
children.text: The Secretary shall allocate the funds so provided in accordance with paragraphs (2), (3), and (4) of this subsection.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), the Secretary shall allocate to each State for administrative costs incurred in any fiscal year in connection with the programs authorized under the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act [ et seq.] or under this chapter, except for the programs authorized under section 13 or 17 of the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act [, 1766] or under , an amount equal to not less than 1 percent and not more than 1½ percent of the funds expended by each State under sections 4 and 11 of the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act [, 1759a] and sections 1772 and 1773 of this title during the second preceding fiscal year.42 U.S.C. 175142 U.S.C. 1761section 1786 of this title42 U.S.C. 1753

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In no case shall the grant available to any State under this paragraph be less than the amount such State was allocated in the fiscal year ending , or $200,000 (as adjusted under clause (ii), whichever is larger.September 30, 19811So in original. Probably should be preceded by an additional closing parenthesis.1

children.label: I
children.text: the value of the index for State and local government purchases, as published by the Bureau of Economic Analysis of the Department of Commerce, for the 12-month period ending June 30 of the second preceding fiscal year; and

children.label: II
children.text: the value of that index for the 12-month period ending June 30 of the preceding fiscal year.

The Secretary shall allocate to each State for its administrative costs incurred under the program authorized by section 17 of the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act [] in any fiscal year an amount, based upon funds expended under that program in the second preceding fiscal year, equal to (A) 20 percent of the first $50,000, (B) 10 percent of the next $100,000, (C) 5 percent of the next $250,000, and (D) 2½ percent of any remaining funds. If an agency in the State other than the State educational agency administers such program, the State shall ensure that an amount equal to no less than the funds due the State under this paragraph is provided to such agency for costs incurred by such agency in administering the program, except as provided in paragraph (5). The Secretary may adjust any State’s allocation to reflect changes in the size of its program.42 U.S.C. 1766

The remaining funds appropriated under this section shall be allocated among the States by the Secretary in amounts the Secretary determines necessary for the improvement in the States of the administration of the programs authorized under the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act [ et seq.] and this chapter, except for , including, but not limited to, improved program integrity and the quality of meals served to children.42 U.S.C. 1751section 1786 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: Not more than 25 percent of the amounts made available to each State under this section for the fiscal year 1991 and 20 percent of the amounts made available to each State under this section for the fiscal year 1992 and for each succeeding fiscal year may remain available for obligation or expenditure in the fiscal year succeeding the fiscal year for which such amounts were appropriated.

children.heading: .—Return to secretary
children.label: i .—Return to secretary
children.text: For each fiscal year, any amounts appropriated that are not obligated or expended during the fiscal year and are not carried over for the succeeding fiscal year under subparagraph (A) shall be returned to the Secretary.

children.heading: .—Reallocation by secretary
children.label: ii .—Reallocation by secretary
children.text: The Secretary shall allocate, for purposes of administrative costs, any remaining amounts among States that demonstrate a need for the amounts.

children.heading: .—Use of administrative funds
children.label: 6 .—Use of administrative funds
children.text: Funds available to a State under this subsection and under section 13(k)(1) of the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act () may be used by the State for the costs of administration of the programs authorized under this chapter (except for the programs authorized under sections 1786 and 1790 of this title) and the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act ( et seq.) without regard to the basis on which the funds were earned and allocated.42 U.S.C. 1761(k)(1)42 U.S.C. 1751

Where the Secretary is responsible for the administration of programs under this chapter or the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act [ et seq.], the amount of funds that would be allocated to the State agency under this section and under section 13(k)(1) of the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act [] shall be retained by the Secretary for the Secretary’s use in the administration of such programs.42 U.S.C. 175142 U.S.C. 1761(k)(1)

In the fiscal year 1991 and each succeeding fiscal year, in accordance with regulations issued by the Secretary, each State shall ensure that the State agency administering the distribution of commodities under programs authorized under this chapter and under the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act [ et seq.] is provided, from funds made available to the State under this subsection, an appropriate amount of funds for administrative costs incurred in distributing such commodities. In developing such regulations, the Secretary may consider the value of commodities provided to the State under this chapter and under the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act.42 U.S.C. 1751

children.label: A
children.text: If the Secretary determines that the administration of any program by a State under this chapter (other than ) or under the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act ( et seq.) (including any requirement to provide sufficient training, technical assistance, and monitoring of the child and adult care food program under section 17 of that Act ()), or compliance with a regulation issued pursuant to either this chapter or such Act, is seriously deficient, and the State fails to correct the deficiency within a specified period of time, the Secretary may withhold from the State some or all of the funds allocated to the State under this section or under section 13(k)(1) or 17 of the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act ( or 1766).section 1786 of this title42 U.S.C. 175142 U.S.C. 176642 U.S.C. 1761(k)(1)

children.label: B
children.text: On a subsequent determination by the Secretary that the administration of any program referred to in subparagraph (A), or compliance with the regulations issued to carry out the program, is no longer seriously deficient and is operated in an acceptable manner, the Secretary may allocate some or all of the funds withheld under such subparagraph.

children.heading: Funds, usage: compensation, benefits, and travel expenses of personnel; support services; office equipment; staff development
children.label: b Funds, usage: compensation, benefits, and travel expenses of personnel; support services; office equipment; staff development
children.text: Funds paid to a State under subsection (a) of this section may be used to pay salaries, including employee benefits and travel expenses, for administrative and supervisory personnel; for support services; for office equipment; and for staff development.

children.heading: Fund adjustment; State administered programs
children.label: c Fund adjustment; State administered programs
children.text: If any State agency agrees to assume responsibility for the administration of food service programs in nonprofit private schools or child care institutions that were previously administered by the Secretary, an appropriate adjustment shall be made in the administrative funds paid under this section to the State not later than the succeeding fiscal year.

children.heading: Unused funds; availability for obligation and expenditure, and reallocation to other States
children.label: d Unused funds; availability for obligation and expenditure, and reallocation to other States
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, funds made available to each State under this section shall remain available for obligation and expenditure by that State during the fiscal year immediately following the fiscal year for which such funds were made available. For each fiscal year the Secretary shall establish a date by which each State shall submit to the Secretary a plan for the disbursement of funds provided under this section for each such year, and the Secretary shall reallocate any unused funds, as evidenced by such plans, to other States as the Secretary considers appropriate.

children.heading: Plans for use of administrative expense funds
children.label: e Plans for use of administrative expense funds

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Each State shall submit to the Secretary for approval by October 1 of the initial fiscal year a plan for the use of State administrative expense funds, including a staff formula for State personnel, system level supervisory and operating personnel, and school level personnel.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: After submitting the initial plan, a State shall be required to submit to the Secretary for approval only a substantive change in the plan.

children.label: i
children.text: monitoring the nutrient content of meals served;

children.label: ii
children.text: training local educational agencies, school food authorities, and schools in how to use technology and information management systems (including verifying eligibility for free or reduced price meals using program participation or income data gathered by State or local agencies); and

children.label: iii
children.text: using electronic data to establish benchmarks to compare and monitor program integrity, program participation, and financial data.

children.heading: Training and technical assistance
children.label: 3 Training and technical assistance
children.text: Each State shall submit to the Secretary for approval a plan describing the manner in which the State intends to implement subsection (g) and section 22(b)(3) of the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act [].42 U.S.C. 1769c(b)(3)

children.heading: State funding requirement
children.label: f State funding requirement
children.text: Payments of funds under this section shall be made only to States that agree to maintain a level of funding out of State revenues, for administrative costs in connection with programs under this chapter (except ) and the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act [ et seq.] (except section 13 of that Act []), not less than the amount expended or obligated in fiscal year 1977, and that agree to participate fully in any studies authorized by the Secretary.section 1786 of this title42 U.S.C. 175142 U.S.C. 1761

children.heading: Professional standards for school food service
children.label: g Professional standards for school food service

children.heading: Criteria for school food service and State agency directors
children.label: 1 Criteria for school food service and State agency directors

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a program of required education, training, and certification for all school food service directors responsible for the management of a school food authority.

children.label: I
children.text: minimum educational requirements necessary to successfully manage the school lunch program established under the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act ( et seq.) and the school breakfast program established by ;42 U.S.C. 1751section 1773 of this title

children.label: II
children.text: minimum program training and certification criteria for school food service directors; and

children.label: III
children.text: minimum periodic training criteria to maintain school food service director certification.

children.heading: School nutrition State agency directors
children.label: B School nutrition State agency directors
children.text: The Secretary shall establish criteria and standards for States to use in the selection of State agency directors with responsibility for the school lunch program established under the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act ( et seq.) and the school breakfast program established by .42 U.S.C. 1751section 1773 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: to establish and manage the program under this paragraph; and

children.label: ii
children.text: to develop voluntary training and certification programs for other school food service workers.

children.heading: School food service directors
children.label: i School food service directors
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a date by which all school food service directors whose local educational agencies are participating in the school lunch program established under the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act ( et seq.) and the school breakfast program established by shall be required to comply with the education, training, and certification criteria established in accordance with subparagraph (A).42 U.S.C. 1751section 1773 of this title

children.heading: School nutrition State agency directors
children.label: ii School nutrition State agency directors
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a date by which all State agencies shall be required to comply with criteria and standards established in accordance with subparagraph (B) for the selection of State agency directors with responsibility for the school lunch program established under the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act ( et seq.) and the school breakfast program established by .42 U.S.C. 1751section 1773 of this title

children.heading: Training and certification of food service personnel
children.label: 2 Training and certification of food service personnel

children.heading: Training for individuals conducting or overseeing administrative procedures
children.label: A Training for individuals conducting or overseeing administrative procedures

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: At least annually, each State shall provide training in administrative practices (including training in application, certification, verification, meal counting, and meal claiming procedures) to local educational agency and school food authority personnel and other appropriate personnel.

children.label: I
children.text: provide training and technical assistance described in clause (i) to the State; or

children.label: II
children.text: at the option of the Secretary, directly provide training and technical assistance described in clause (i).

children.heading: Required participation
children.label: iii Required participation
children.text: In accordance with procedures established by the Secretary, each local educational agency or school food authority shall ensure that an individual conducting or overseeing administrative procedures described in clause (i) receives training at least annually, unless determined otherwise by the Secretary.

children.heading: Training and certification of all local food service personnel
children.label: B Training and certification of all local food service personnel
I
children.text: the accuracy of approvals for free and reduced price meals; and

children.label: II
children.text: the identification of reimbursable meals at the point of service.

children.label: I
children.text: to ensure program compliance and integrity; and

children.label: II
children.text: to demonstrate competence in the training provided under clauses (i) and (iii).

children.label: I
children.text: nutrition;

children.label: II
children.text: food allergies, including information on the best practices to prevent, recognize, and respond to food-related allergic reactions;

children.label: III
children.text: health and food safety standards and methodologies; and

children.label: IV
children.text: any other appropriate topics, as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: on , $5,000,000; andOctober 1, 2010

children.label: ii
children.text: on each October 1 thereafter, $1,000,000.

children.heading: Receipt and acceptance
children.label: B Receipt and acceptance
children.text: The Secretary shall be entitled to receive, shall accept, and shall use to carry out this subsection the funds transferred under subparagraph (A), without further appropriation.

children.heading: Funding for training and administrative reviews
children.label: h Funding for training and administrative reviews

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: On , and on each October 1 thereafter, out of any funds in the Treasury not otherwise appropriated, the Secretary of the Treasury shall transfer to the Secretary of Agriculture to carry out this subsection $4,000,000, to remain available until expended.October 1, 2004

children.heading: Receipt and acceptance
children.label: B Receipt and acceptance
children.text: The Secretary shall be entitled to receive, shall accept, and shall use to carry out this subsection the funds transferred under subparagraph (A), without further appropriation.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), the Secretary shall use funds provided under this subsection to assist States in carrying out subsection (g) and administrative reviews of selected local educational agencies carried out under section 22 of the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act ().42 U.S.C. 1769c

children.heading: Exception
children.label: B Exception
children.text: The Secretary may retain a portion of the amount provided to cover costs of activities carried out by the Secretary in lieu of the State.

children.heading: Allocation
children.label: 3 Allocation
children.text: The Secretary shall allocate funds provided under this subsection to States based on the number of local educational agencies that have demonstrated a high level of, or a high risk for, administrative error, as determined by the Secretary, taking into account the requirements established by the Child Nutrition and WIC Reauthorization Act of 2004 and the amendments made by that Act.

children.heading: Reallocation
children.label: 4 Reallocation
children.text: The Secretary may reallocate, to carry out this section, any amounts made available to carry out this subsection that are not obligated or expended, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Each State shall submit to the Secretary, for approval by the Secretary, an amendment to the plan required by subsection (e) that describes the manner in which funds provided under this section will be used for technology and information management systems.

children.label: A
children.text: monitoring the nutrient content of meals served;

children.label: i
children.text: menu planning;

children.label: ii
children.text: collection of point-of-sale data; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the processing of applications for free and reduced price meals; and

children.label: C
children.text: using electronic data to establish benchmarks to compare and monitor program integrity, program participation, and financial data across schools and school food authorities.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to the availability of funds made available under paragraph (4) to carry out this paragraph, the Secretary shall, on a competitive basis, provide funds to States to be used to provide grants to local educational agencies, school food authorities, and schools to defray the cost of purchasing or upgrading technology and information management systems for use in programs authorized by this chapter (other than ) and the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act ( et seq.).section 1786 of this title42 U.S.C. 1751

children.label: i
children.text: processing and verification of applications for free and reduced price meals;

children.label: ii
children.text: integration of menu planning, production, and serving data to monitor compliance with section 9(f)(1) of the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act (); and42 U.S.C. 1758(f)(1)

children.label: iii
children.text: compatibility with statewide reporting systems.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 4 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this subsection such sums as are necessary for each of fiscal years 2010 through 2015, to remain available until expended.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: j Authorization of appropriations
children.text: For the fiscal year beginning , and each succeeding fiscal year ending before , there are hereby authorized to be appropriated such sums as may be necessary for the purposes of this section.October 1, 1977October 1, 2015
